channel.title: In Simple Terms with Satish
channel.description: In Simple Terms Daily Tech Briefing: technical rabbit holes explained simply for builders.
channel.itunes:summary: In Simple Terms Daily Tech Briefing: technical rabbit holes explained simply for builders.
channel.itunes:author: Satish Choudhary

item.title: NLWeb and AI-readable websites
item.description:
In Simple Terms daily tech briefing. Today's concrete rabbit hole is NLWeb: a protocol and reference implementation for turning websites into natural-language interfaces that both humans and AI agents can query.
Top signals: - Microsoft introduced NLWeb on May 19, 2025 as an open project for natural-language interfaces on websites. - The current NLWeb spec is no longer just "site chat"; it defines query structure, async `await`, transport bindings, and output formats including `chatgpt_app`. - The NLWeb docs explicitly build on Schema.org, JSON-LD, and streaming patterns instead of inventing an isolated AI stack. Focus: protocols, runtimes, architecture, developer tools, AI infrastructure, retrieval, databases, observability, local AI, security, and tool systems.
item.itunes:subtitle: Retrieval and data in simple terms
item.itunes:summary:
In Simple Terms daily tech briefing. Today's concrete rabbit hole is NLWeb: a protocol and reference implementation for turning websites into natural-language interfaces that both humans and AI agents can query.
Top signals: - Microsoft introduced NLWeb on May 19, 2025 as an open project for natural-language interfaces on websites. - The current NLWeb spec is no longer just "site chat"; it defines query structure, async `await`, transport bindings, and output formats including `chatgpt_app`. - The NLWeb docs explicitly build on Schema.org, JSON-LD, and streaming patterns instead of inventing an isolated AI stack. Focus: protocols, runtimes, architecture, developer tools, AI infrastructure, retrieval, databases, observability, local AI, security, and tool systems.
